Enumeration.info_blurb: Enumerate API Gateway gateways, APIs, and API configs, with optional direct targeting by ID and TestIamPermissions.
Enumerate API Keys for the selected project (global location).

Unauthenticated.info_blurb: MIT-ported gmapsapiscanner-style module to enumerate unauthenticated Google Maps and related API scope for an exposed Google API key.
Exploit an exposed Gemini API key by enumerating Gemini files and cached contents, then exercising the token-counting and embeddings REST endpoints.
Exploit an exposed Vertex AI express-mode API key by discovering working publisher models and opening an interactive prompt shell.

Enumeration.info_blurb: Enumerate App Engine application, services, versions, and instances.
Enumerate Artifact Registry repositories, packages, and versions from one service module. Dependency layers use cache automatically or accept explicit parent flags.
Enumerate Batch jobs.
Enumerate BigQuery datasets and tables, with optional direct targeting by dataset or table ID.
Enumerate Cloud Bigtable instances, clusters, and tables.
Enumerate Cloud Build triggers, builds, and connections across regions for the selected project.
Enumerate Cloud Composer environments across regions and optionally download software config overrides from retrieved metadata.
Enumerate Cloud DNS managed zones and record sets. Use --zones and/or --record-sets to control depth; record sets can be filtered by --record-type.
Enumerate Cloud Functions resources from one service module. Supports regional scoping, source download, external curl checks, and TestIamPermissions.

Exploit.info_blurb: Upload or modify function code. Subsequently invoke it to get the Oauth token for a attached service account. Can auto-assume new role if desired. Can also be used to standalone call a function.

Unauthenticated.info_blurb: Based off Rhino Security code here: https://github.com/RhinoSecurityLabs/GCPBucketBrute. Same concept but modified slightly to brute forcue functions by URLs unauth.

Enumeration.info_blurb: Enumerate Cloud KMS keyrings/keys/versions across locations (defaults to locations/-). Use --get to fetch per-key metadata.
Enumerate Cloud Run services and jobs across locations (defaults to locations/-). Use --get to enrich per-resource metadata.
Enumerate Cloud SQL instances, cached instance configs, databases, and users using the SQL Admin API.
Enumerate Cloud Storage resources from one service module. Use flags like --buckets, --blobs, and --hmac-keys to control which resource types are collected.

Exploit.info_blurb: Authenticated upload of a local file or STDIN to a Cloud Storage Bucket. You can specify the arbitrary path you want to send the file contents/STDIN to. See wiki for more details.

Create a new HMAC Key tied to a service account. Subsequently use this key to try to enumerate/download Cloud Bucket Data using the SigV4 XML API. The module can also activate/deactivate existing keys

Unauthenticated.info_blurb: A copy of Rhino Security GCP Brute tool. Will brute force GCP buckets given a keyterm and can check for anonymous permissions. Should not leverage credentials to try authenticated permisision checks, although that might be added in the future

Enumeration.info_blurb: Enumerate Cloud Tasks queues and tasks across supported locations, with optional HTTP task request sample downloads.
Enumerate Compute Engine load balancing resources including backend buckets/services, forwarding rules, URL maps, and target proxies.
Enumerate Compute Engine networking resources including VPCs, subnetworks, firewalls, routers/NAT, VPN, attachments, and interconnect surfaces.
Enumerate Compute Engine resource-plane objects including projects, instances, disks, images, snapshots, machine images, node resources, reservations, and instance groups.

Exploit.info_blurb: Upload the public portion of an SSH key either to a projec tor instance. This might allow for SSH access depending on if no other restrictions are in place. Project edits will impact all instances in the project. Ergo uploading SSH keys to a project grants SSH access to all instances

Create an instance or edit an existing one (through a restart) and upload a startup script that will exfiltrate the compute metadata to a URL of the caller's choice. These need to be manually added via `creds add` in gcpwn. Note a service account needs to be attached to the compute instance requiring those permissions.

Enumeration.info_blurb: Call all enumeration modules defined in gcpwn. Can add flags like --iam to do all IAM TestIAMPermission checks and --all-permissions to do ~9000 permission checks at the project/folder/org level. Can also add the --download flag to try to do mass dowload while enumerating

Enumerate IAM policy bindings across already-enumerated resources (org/folder/project plus service resources). Uses Cloud Asset Inventory when available and falls back to per-service APIs for key services (Storage/BigQuery/Compute/Functions/Secrets/KMS/Run/PubSub/Spanner/Service Directory/Cloud Tasks/Artifact Registry).

Exploit.info_blurb: Unified SetIamPolicy helper. Prompts for the service/resource (or accepts flags) and adds a binding for a given member+role (defaults to session email + a service-appropriate admin role). Supports Resource Manager, Storage, Compute, Functions, Secrets, Cloud Run, Artifact Registry, KMS, Pub/Sub, Spanner, Service Directory, and Cloud Tasks.

Process.info_blurb: Review org-wide security issues that might arise mainly with roles/permissions. Export the data to a csv or txt with --csv or --txt respectively. Note this will handle both convenience and custom roles in addition to normal predefined GCP roles, but not constraints or anything more advanced.

Enumeration.info_blurb: Enumerate Firestore databases via the Firestore Admin API.
Enumerate GKE clusters across locations (defaults to locations/-). Use --get to fetch per-cluster metadata.
Enumerate Google Workspace / Cloud Identity groups and memberships. Uses directory customer ID (directoryCustomerId) where available.
Enumerate IAM resources from one service module. Supports service accounts, custom roles, policy bindings, and Workload Identity pools/providers/service-account IAM policies.

Exploit.info_blurb: Generate an access token for a service account and assume it within GCPwn (--assume) to pivot to a different user. Note this also handles implicit delegation with the --delegation flag relying on you to either supply the role chain or checking whats been enumerated thus far to help you build the delegation chain.

Generate an account key for a service account and assume it within GCPwn (--assume) to pivot to a different user. Note unlike the access token which usually lasts for around 1 hour, a service key can last for much longer and is a much more permanent pivot.

Enumeration.info_blurb: Enumerate Memorystore Redis resources from one service module.

Processing.info_blurb: Build OpenGraph data from cached workspace tables and export BloodHound-compatible JSON output.
Sync OpenGraph custom node color/image metadata to a BloodHound custom-node endpoint.

Enumeration.info_blurb: Enumerate Pub/Sub topics, subscriptions, schemas, and snapshots, with optional direct targeting by full resource name.

Enumerate projects/folders/organizations. Pass in --iam to perform testIAMPermissions on each resource. Adding --all-permissions will enumerate ~9000 individual permission in batches and is the most comprehensive.
Enumerate Secret Manager resources from one service module. Use flags like --secrets, --versions, and --values to control depth.
Enumerate Service Directory namespaces, services, and endpoints across regions (defaults to locations/-).
Enumerate Storage Transfer resources from one service module.
Enumerate AgentPlatform (aiplatform) datasets, endpoints, models, featurestores, entity types, feature groups, feature online stores, feature views, reasoning engines, and notebook runtime templates.
